label: Public health
matchingRationale: The product set maps directly to CDC prevention and after-bite guidance, with prevention steps shown before affiliate links.
title: Tick-bite ER visits are higher than usual
summary: CDC reported on April 23, 2026 that emergency room visits for tick bites are higher than normal in many parts of the United States. The useful response is prevention-first: EPA-registered repellents, protective clothing or treated gear, tick checks, and prompt removal if a tick is attached.
whyNow: CDC says weekly ER-visit rates are the highest for this time of year since 2017 in all U.S. regions except the South Central region.
imageAlt: A sunlit forest trail with dense grass and brush where ticks may live
editorialSafeguards: Primary event source is CDC, with EPA used only for repellent-selection evidence.
Recommendations are prevention, checking, removal, or yard-risk reduction steps tied to source guidance.
Medical guidance stays with CDC, and the shopping copy avoids alarmist or treatment language.

storySignals.label: Highest weekly ER-visit rate for this point in the season
CDC reports elevated tick-bite ER visits across much of the U.S.
Prevention, checks, and quick removal matter more than alarm

nextSteps.title: Before going outside
nextSteps.body: Use an EPA-registered repellent and consider 0.5% permethrin-treated boots, socks, clothing, or camping gear where appropriate.

nextSteps.title: After coming inside
nextSteps.body: Check clothing, pets, and skin after time in grassy, brushy, or wooded areas; showering soon after being outdoors can help with tick checks.

nextSteps.title: If a tick is attached
nextSteps.body: Remove it promptly with clean, fine-tipped tweezers, then follow CDC after-bite guidance and watch for symptoms.

recommendations.title: EPA-registered tick repellent
recommendations.matchReason: CDC recommends EPA-registered repellents with active ingredients such as DEET, picaridin, IR3535, OLE, PMD, or 2-undecanone.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Repellent is a direct prevention step before outdoor exposure in grassy, brushy, or wooded areas.
recommendations.destinationJustification: This is a category-level prevention item, so a direct ASIN is not reliable without PA-API matching.

recommendations.title: Permethrin-treated clothing or gear
recommendations.matchReason: CDC says 0.5% permethrin can be used on boots, clothing, and camping gear, or users can buy pre-treated gear.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Treated footwear, clothing, or gear can reduce exposure before hikes, yard work, camping, or outdoor jobs.
recommendations.destinationJustification: This is a category-level gear recommendation until PA-API can resolve a compliant direct product URL.

recommendations.title: Fine-tipped tweezers
recommendations.matchReason: CDC says attached ticks should be removed as soon as possible, and clean fine-tipped tweezers work well for removal.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Tweezers support the after-exposure step CDC names, without implying treatment or diagnosis.
recommendations.destinationJustification: This remains category-level until PA-API provides an exact direct product match.

recommendations.title: Yard tick-risk reduction
recommendations.matchReason: CDC lists mowing, clearing tall grass, removing leaf litter, and creating barriers as ways to reduce tick exposure near the home.
recommendations.whyItHelps: This is a non-affiliate service/action path for readers who need environmental steps more than products.

label: Weather readiness
matchingRationale: The recommendation set is limited to preparedness basics supported by NWS and Ready.gov, with no storm-intensity predictions.
title: Hurricane Preparedness Week is underway
summary: The National Weather Service preparedness calendar lists Hurricane Preparedness Week for May 3-9, 2026 in several hurricane-prone regions. The practical move is to refresh alerts, evacuation plans, water, power, medication, documents, and pet supplies before the season accelerates.
whyNow: Atlantic hurricane season begins June 1, and official preparedness campaigns are running now so households can build kits before storms are approaching.
imageAlt: A dramatic storm cloud over a road at dusk
editorialSafeguards: Preparedness recommendations are evergreen basics, not storm-specific predictions.
The commerce set is limited to alerts, power, and water-storage needs tied to official guidance.
Official guidance links remain visible beside monetized destinations.

recommendations.title: NOAA weather radio
recommendations.matchReason: Preparedness guidance commonly includes a battery-powered or hand-crank radio so alerts still work during outages.
recommendations.whyItHelps: A radio is useful when mobile networks or home power fail and households still need official alerts.
recommendations.destinationJustification: Category-level preparedness item; use direct PA-API product URLs only after exact model matching.

recommendations.title: Portable power and charging
recommendations.matchReason: Storm prep should account for phone charging, flashlights, radios, and essential devices when electricity is interrupted.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Backup charging supports communication and alerts without implying a specific product is required.
recommendations.destinationJustification: Category-level backup-power guidance; exact direct products require availability and safety checks.

recommendations.title: Water storage and basic supplies
recommendations.matchReason: Preparedness campaigns emphasize having enough water, non-perishable food, medication, and household basics for several days.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Water storage is a practical kit item that can be bought ahead of a storm instead of during last-minute demand.
recommendations.destinationJustification: Category-level kit item; direct-product matching is deferred until commerce API verification.

label: Air quality
matchingRationale: The guide uses EPA-linked awareness resources and keeps product claims to monitoring and filtration, not health outcomes.
title: Air Quality Awareness Week begins May 4
summary: Air Quality Awareness Week runs May 4-8, 2026, with EPA-linked resources focused on wildland smoke, asthma, indoor air quality, and knowing local air conditions. The practical response is to monitor AQI and improve filtration where it is useful.
whyNow: May 4 starts the week with wildland fires and smoke, and federal/state resources point users toward AirNow and smoke-readiness guidance.
imageAlt: A home air purifier in a bright room
editorialSafeguards: The event source is a state public-health page tied to EPA awareness resources.
Recommendations focus on checking AQI and filtration planning, not medical claims.
AirNow is included as a non-affiliate service path before purchase decisions.

recommendations.title: HEPA room air purifier
recommendations.matchReason: For indoor air quality planning, a properly sized purifier can help reduce airborne particles in a room when used as directed.
recommendations.whyItHelps: A purifier is relevant only when indoor filtration is useful for the room, household, and local AQI context.
recommendations.destinationJustification: Category-level filtration item; direct-product matching requires room-size and availability checks.

recommendations.title: MERV-rated HVAC filters
recommendations.matchReason: Filtration planning is one of the practical household steps people revisit when smoke, pollen, or poor AQI is in the news.
recommendations.whyItHelps: A filter can be relevant when compatible with the home system and changed according to manufacturer guidance.
recommendations.destinationJustification: Category-level filter recommendation because exact size and HVAC compatibility vary by home.

recommendations.title: AirNow AQI checks
recommendations.matchReason: EPA-linked AirNow resources help users check local AQI and smoke information before changing outdoor plans.
recommendations.whyItHelps: Checking local AQI is a non-affiliate first step that can prevent unnecessary product purchases.
